description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone fragment with a roughly triangular profile and a rugged, uneven surface. The stone displays a mix of gray, brown, and reddish hues, with visible patches of rust-like discoloration and some greenish spots, suggesting mineral deposits or weathering. The surface is coarse and pitted, with jagged edges and a granular texture, indicating it has been exposed to the elements for some time. There are hand-written markings in black ink on one face, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, irregular stone fragment with gray, brown, and reddish tones, featuring coarse texture and weathered markings.
description_view_0: The main face shows a broad, flat surface with visible black ink markings and a mottled gray-brown coloration.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the stone's thickness, jagged edge, and rusty discoloration along one side.
description_view_2: The top view reveals a rough, uneven surface with prominent brown and reddish weathering, lacking any visible markings.
description_view_3: The reverse side displays a more rounded, pitted surface with reddish patches and less visible textural detail.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, or landscaped garden
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled and speckled with irregular patches of rust, gray, and green; no artificial carvings or regular veining
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible rust and mineral stains, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to moisture
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
